betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Spatial Components§f:
Spatial Components (appliedenergistics2:material:32 for 2³, appliedenergistics2:material:60 for 16³) are the §einternal storage elements of Spatial Storage Cells§f. They store §bblock space§f instead of items or fluids.

§6Crafting Recipe (materials/spatial_component_2.json)§f:
The 2³ Spatial Component (appliedenergistics2:material:32) is crafted with:
- 1× Glowstone Dust (vanilla) at center
- 1× Fluix Pearl (appliedenergistics2:material:12) — refined Fluix form
- 1× Engineering Processor (appliedenergistics2:material:17) at center
- 4× Iron Ingot (vanilla, in corners)
- Yields 1× §62³§r Spatial Component

§6Spatial Component Tiers§f:
- §b2³ Spatial Component§f: stores a 2×2×2 cube of blocks (8 block types)
- §b16³ Spatial Component§f: stores a 16×16×16 cube of blocks (4096 block types)
- §b128³ Spatial Component§f: stores a 128×128×128 cube of blocks (massive, late-game)

§6Direct Descendants of 2³ Spatial Component (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 2 items that use the 2³ Component:
- §62³§r Spatial Storage Cell (the housing + component combo)
- §916³§r Spatial Component (the next tier)

§6What Spatial Storage Does§f:
A Spatial Storage Cell allows you to:
- §bStore arbitrary blocks§f: any block in the world, in a fixed volume
- §bRetrieve them later§f: the cell reconstructs the exact 3D arrangement
- §bPreserve tile entities§f: chests, machines, etc. are stored with their contents
- §bUse as portable base§f: pick up the cell, move it, redeploy the base

§6Common Uses§f:
- §bMobile base§f: store a small base in a cell, move it anywhere
- §bQuarry transport§f: store a quarry output and move it to a processing area
- §bBackup§f: create a backup of important structures (homes, factories, mob farms)
- §bStorage for machines§f: machines can be moved without breaking/replacing

§6Crafting Ingredients (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 3 ingredient items in the 2³ Component recipe:
- Glowstone Dust (vanilla)
- Fluix Pearl (AE2)
- Engineering Processor (AE2)

§6Why Spatial Components Matter§f:
Spatial Components are the §emost expensive single item in AE2§f. The 128³ component requires Glowstone Blocks, Fluix Pearls, and a 16³ component. They are the ultimate "build it once, use it anywhere" tool.

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30149 (Printed Circuits & Processors) — the Spatial Component recipe requires an Engineering Processor.

§eTip§f: For most players, a §b16³ cell is the sweet spot§f. It can store a small house (16×16 footprint) with all its contents.

§eTip§f: Always test your spatial storage with a §bsmall backup first§f (e.g. a 2³ cell with a chest). Once you trust the technology, scale up.

§cWarning§f: Spatial storage of hostile-mob spawners is possible but can cause issues. Avoid storing mob farms in spatial cells — the spawn logic may break on redeployment.
